title: get() name parameter: ignored by all backends, used as cache key in CachingStore
description: Every backend ignores the name parameter in get(name) and put(name, _). The SecretStore trait declares get(&self, name: &str) as if backends support multi-secret fetching, but all implementations are single-secret (the secret is fixed at from_uri time). CachingStore uses name as a cache key, so calling store.get('a') and store.get('b') on the same backend populates the cache with two entries for the same underlying value. The trait contract should document whether name is expected to be used, or the parameter should be removed. A well-intentioned contributor implementing a new backend will not know whether to use name or ignore it.
